Remove Pillow & protobuf from .buildkite/run-cpu-test.sh, as they are already installed by dockerfile.cpu.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix default weight loading for scalars
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
It is pretty easy to run into issues when loading scalar weights from checkpoints into parameters, since they can have no shape in some cases, resulting in errors like
  File "/home/mgoin/code/vllm/vllm/model_executor/model_loader/weight_utils.py", line 525, in default_weight_loader
    assert param

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Harmless] Fix hardcoded float16 dtype for model_is_embedding
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
FIX #7561
On main you would see a concerning but harmless message about casting to float16 for models that are bfloat16:
vllm serve meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
...
WARNING 08-15 21:06:16 config.py:1514] Casting torch.bfloat16 to torch.float16.
INFO 08-15 21:06:16 api_server.py:111] Multipr

vllm.title: [Misc] Add quantization config support for speculative model.
vllm.author: ShangmingCai
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Currently, we are not able to specify the quantization method for the draft model. Yet when we set up an INT4 gptq-based draft model, vllm will automatically use gptq_marlin to config the quantization and may raise some errors such as
ValueError: Marlin does not support weight_bits = uint4b8. Only t

vllm.title: [Feature]: Add OpenAI server prompt_logprobs support #6508
vllm.author: gnpinkert
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This commit adds a prompt_logprobs option in the extra body field of the chat completions API. When set to true, it will return the log probabilities of the decoded input tokens.
This option was not included in the streaming API. This decision was made since streaming is meant for real time feedback

vllm.title: [CI] Move quantization cpu offload tests out of fastcheck
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Fastcheck was taking too long because of all the quantization cpu offloading tests I added to "Basic Correctness". This moves them under tests/quantization/test_cpu_offloading.py

vllm.title: [Misc/Testing] Use `torch.testing.assert_close`
vllm.author: jon-chuang
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Easier to debug and diagnose issues with accuracy
For example, while correctness testing, small error may mean one just needs to change rtol/atol due to lower precision / quantization.
Example Output:
AssertionError: Tensor-likes are not close!

Mismatched elements: 100 / 100 (100.0%)
Greatest absol

vllm.author: youkaichao

vllm.title: [Core] Use uvloop with zmq-decoupled front-end
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt: It's faster than the native python asyncio event loop implementation. uvicorn already uses this but we need to enable it explicitly when starting the zmq RPC server based engine.
